'''Oseon 5631''' was an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] located in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality's]] [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]]. The renowned [[Musician/Legends|singer]] [[Ema Slake]] maintained an [[Ema Slake's estate|estate]], the most luxurious of the Oseon [[Star system/Legends|system]] asteroid habitats by [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], on Oseon 5631. At one point, Slake's agent, [[Sosten Freet]], [[Thief/Legends|stole]] several of the singer's trophies, but they were eventually discovered in the agent's own Oseon 5631 [[Freet's home|residence]] by a group of investigators.

==Description==
Oseon 5631 was an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] located in the [[Oseon asteroid belt]], a part of the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]].<ref name="Gamer" /> It was situated in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]], a part of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA Oseon" /> Along with the rest of the Oseon [[Star system/Legends|system]], Oseon 5631 [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Oseon sun]]<ref name="LCFO" /> and was connected by the [[Falko Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] to the [[Arleen system/Legends|Arleen system]], while the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route known as the [[Cadma Conduit]] linked it to the [[Erilnar system/Legends|Erilnar system]] and the<ref name="TEA Oseon" /> [[Cadma sector/Legends|Cadma sector's]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> [[Dagelin Minor system/Legends|Dagelin Minor]] and [[Simbarc system/Legends|Simbarc systems]].<ref name="TEA Oseon" />

==History==
{{RPGseed}}
[[Sosten Freet]], the agent of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-renowned [[Musician/Legends|singer]] [[Ema Slake]], at one point [[Thief/Legends|stole]] more than half of the artist's [[Silicate/Legends|silicate]] trophies, which Freet then stored at his [[Freet's home|residence]] on Oseon 5631 in crates that were ready to be shipped off. To eliminate himself as a suspect, Freet framed Slake's former [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]] [[Director/Legends|director]], [[O'lif Khaz]], by planting one of the missing trophies at the latter's [[Khaz's apartment|apartment]] on [[Oseon 8920]] and eventually had him arrested.<ref name="Gamer" />

Upon leaving that asteroid, however, the group of individuals Freet had hired to investigate Khaz were captured by thugs affiliated with Slake's retired manager, [[Phaldri Nex]]. When Nex learned of Freet's involvement in the affair, the former became suspicious and offered to liberate the investigators if they broke into Freet's home on Oseon 5631. The group agreed and discovered the stolen trophies there, after which Freet was apprehended and both the culprit and the trophies were delivered to Slake's [[Ema Slake's estate|estate]] on the same asteroid.<ref name="Gamer" />
{{Endgame}}

==Locations==
On Oseon 5631, the [[Albingi]] Ema Slake maintained an estate that was the most luxurious of all the asteroid habitats in the Oseon system<ref name="Gamer" /> by [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]].<ref name="Date">{{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}} establishes that [[Ema Slake's estate]] was the most luxurious of the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]] [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] habitats thirty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Oseon|destruction]] of [[Oseon 5792]], which, since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the latter to [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], translates to [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]].</ref> The entire asteroid served as a showcase of the singer's success, and it featured three [[Amusement park|theme parks]], a [[Museum/Legends|museum]] that commemorated Slake's [[Music/Legends|songs]] and life, and a [[Library/Legends|library]] that collected a quarter of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy's]] musical compositions. The [[Advozse/Legends|Advozse]] Sosten Freet also kept a residence on Oseon 5631.<ref name="Gamer" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:A_Campaign_Guide_to_the_Centrality_G5.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Oseon 8920 was introduced in the article "A Campaign Guide to the Centrality."]]
Oseon 5631 was mentioned in the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article "[[A Campaign Guide to the Centrality]]," which was authored by [[Michael Kogge]] and was published in [[Star Wars Gamer 5|''Star Wars Gamer'' 5]]<ref name="Gamer" /> on [[July 24]], [[2001]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010627.html|text=''Gamer'' #5. A Domain of Evil it is.}}</ref> In "A Campaign Guide to the Centrality," the asteroid serves as a setting for a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure hook, which this article assumes plays out as described.<ref name="Gamer" />

"[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed the Oseon system, and therefore Oseon 5631, in the [[Zebitrope sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently overrode the ''Voyages SF'' placement by establishing that the Oseon system was situated in grid square T-8 as part of the Centrality.<ref name="TEA Oseon" />
